title: Nonzero small amounts can render as 0
reasoning: For bigint inputs, the component converts the exact decimal string from formatEther into a JavaScript number and formats it with at most 4 fractional digits. Any nonzero value below 0.00005 BDAG, such as 1n wei formatted as 0.000000000000000001, is displayed as "0". In an escrow UI this can misrepresent a nonzero on-chain amount as zero.
reproduction: Render <AmountDisplay amount={1n} />; the displayed amount is "0 BDAG" even though the input is nonzero.
recommendation: Keep bigint formatting in string/decimal space and handle nonzero values below the display precision explicitly, for example by rendering "<0.0001" or by preserving enough significant digits for tiny values.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were provided for this component, and there is no evidence of edge-case coverage for tiny nonzero bigint amounts.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test that renders amount={1n} and asserts the UI does not display the numeric value as exactly "0".
minimumFixScope: Update bigint formatting in frontend/src/components/ui/amount-display.tsx and add a focused test for tiny nonzero bigint values.
